name: Tradervue Analytics
description_short: Tradervue is a professional trade journaling and analytics platform used by 207,000+ traders, offering 100+ reports, 200+ broker integrations, and social trade sharing.
description_long:
Tradervue Analytics is one of the most established trade journaling platforms in the industry, founded in 2011 and now serving over 207,000 traders worldwide. The platform focuses on helping active traders systematically analyze their performance through deep analytics, automatic broker imports, and a customizable tagging system that makes it easy to identify patterns, bad habits, and edges in trading behavior.

At the core of Tradervue's value proposition is its analytics engine, which includes over 100 built-in reports covering P&L by tag, time of day, instrument, setup type, and more. The platform supports auto-import from 200+ brokers and trading platforms — including Interactive Brokers, Charles Schwab, DAS Trader, and Lightspeed — with a generic import format for unsupported brokers. Charts are powered by TradingView with entry and exit points automatically marked across multiple timeframes. Risk analysis tools including MFE/MAE (Maximum Favorable/Adverse Excursion) and efficiency metrics help traders understand the quality of their trade execution beyond just P&L.

Tradervue also offers a unique social component where traders can publish trades to a public feed, with the option to keep P&L figures private. This community aspect is a genuine differentiator, useful for traders who want to learn from peers or build a following around their methodology. The Gold plan adds exit analysis, advanced filtering, commissions and fees tracking, and dedicated risk tracking tools for traders managing larger positions.

Despite its analytical depth, Tradervue has notable weaknesses heading into 2026. The interface is widely considered dated compared to newer competitors, there is no native mobile app, and the platform has not yet introduced AI-powered analysis or pattern detection that rivals like TraderSync are offering. Multiple users have also reported billing issues and a difficult cancellation process. These factors make Tradervue best suited for data-driven, high-volume traders who prioritize analytical depth and broad broker compatibility over modern UX and AI-assisted insights.
pros: 100+ built-in reports provide industry-leading analytical depth
Auto-import from 200+ brokers and trading platforms
Unique social trade sharing with privacy controls
MFE/MAE and risk-adjusted performance metrics on Gold plan
cons: No AI-powered analysis or pattern detection despite competitor pressure
No native mobile app — web-only with poor mobile browser experience
Dated interface compared to newer competitors
Reported billing issues and difficult cancellation process
best_for: active day traders with high trade volume
traders wanting broad broker import compatibility
traders interested in social trade sharing
